text:
Once upon an autumn twilight, there lived a shy squirrel named Willow. She lived deep in Whispering Woods, under trees whose leaves danced with fireflies. Willow liked whispers more than shouts. She'd weave fragrant needles into intricate designs on her nest.

One breezy afternoon, Willow found a treasure. A lone crystal eyedstone. She pondered how it could ever talk. Maybe it liked quiet gardens just like her.

Night came, stars shyly peeked through owl owls. Willow burrowed deeper in mossy earth. She imagined her stone friend visiting.

Morning star peeked over. "Hello, Willow." It seemed amused.

"I'm amused you could visit."

"I dream. Of sun dapples." It winked.

Days passed. Willow watched leaves change. She collected sweet dewdrops.

One day, starfall! Willow scurried up.

Stars twinkled. "Thank you."

"Proud?"

"I'm muddy. But I'll remember."

Rain pattered home
